description: This command sets or queries the vertical scale for the specified analog channel. The channel is specified by x.
shortDescription: Set or query channel vertical scale (volts per division)

arguments.name: scale
arguments.description: Vertical scale in volts per division.

description: This command sets or queries the vertical offset for the specified analog channel.
shortDescription: Set or query channel vertical offset

description: This command sets or queries the vertical position for the specified analog channel. Position is in divisions from the center of the screen.
shortDescription: Set or query channel vertical position (divisions from center)

description: This command sets or queries the selectable low-pass bandwidth limit filter for the specified channel. The actual bandwidth depends on the attached probe.
shortDescription: Set or query channel bandwidth limit

arguments.name: bandwidth
arguments.description: Bandwidth limit in Hz. Use FULl for full bandwidth.

description: This command sets or queries the input attenuator coupling setting for the specified channel.
shortDescription: Set or query channel coupling (AC/DC/DCREJect)

description: This command sets or queries the invert state for the specified channel.
shortDescription: Set or query channel inversion (ON/OFF)

description: This command sets or queries the user label for the specified channel.
shortDescription: Set or query channel label name

description: This command sets or queries the deskew time for the specified channel.
shortDescription: Set or query channel deskew time

description: This command sets or queries the vertical termination for the specified analog channel. The channel is specified by x. The available arguments depend on the instrument model and the attached accessories.
shortDescription: Set or query analog channel termination

arguments.name: termination_ohms
arguments.description: Channel input resistance in ohms. Typical values are 50 or 1000000 depending on the model and accessories.

queryResponse.format: Returns the current channel termination in ohms.

examples.description: Might return CH2:TERMINATION 50.0E+0, indicating that channel 2 is set to 50 ohms.

backwardCompatibility.notes: Added as a manual override because the upstream vendor extract embeds this command in a malformed example block.

notes: Available arguments depend on the instrument model and attached accessories.
50 ohms and 1000000 ohms are the common supported values on analog channels.

description: This command sets or queries the MAC length value to use when searching on an AutoEthernet bus signal.
shortDescription: Set or query AutoEthernet MAC length search value

arguments.name: mac_length_value
arguments.description: Binary MAC length value to use for the search.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract truncates the trailing VALue token.
Use with the matching AutoEthernet MAC length search condition.

description: This command sets or queries the binary data string used for eSPI searching when the trigger condition is Virtual wire Index.
shortDescription: Set or query eSPI virtual wire index search value

arguments.name: index_value
arguments.description: Binary index value used for the virtual wire search.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract truncates the trailing VALue token.

description: This command sets or queries the binary data string used for eSPI searching when the trigger condition is Virtual wire Status.
shortDescription: Set or query eSPI virtual wire status search value

arguments.name: status_value
arguments.description: Binary status value used for the virtual wire search.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract truncates the trailing VALue token.

description: This command sets or queries the length of the network variable data string in bytes to be used for EtherCAT bus searching.
shortDescription: Set or query EtherCAT network variable data size

arguments.name: data_size_bytes
arguments.description: Length of the network variable data string in bytes.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract splits the DATa token with bad whitespace.

description: This command specifies the binary string used for Manchester searching if the trigger condition is DATA.
shortDescription: Set or query Manchester packet-off-data search value

arguments.name: data_value
arguments.description: Binary Manchester data string to match.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract breaks the VALue token with embedded whitespace.

description: This command sets or queries the DDR read/write data high reference level value when the search type is DDR READWRITE.
shortDescription: Set or query DDR read/write data high reference level

arguments.name: reference_level_high
arguments.description: High reference level for DDR read/write data.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract does not preserve a clean canonical header for this reference-level command.

description: This command sets or queries the DDR read/write data low reference level value when the search type is DDR READWRITE.
shortDescription: Set or query DDR read/write data low reference level

arguments.name: reference_level_low
arguments.description: Low reference level for DDR read/write data.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract does not preserve a clean canonical header for this reference-level command.

description: This command sets or queries the DDR read/write reference level strobe high value when the search type is DDR READWRITE.
shortDescription: Set or query DDR read/write strobe high reference level

arguments.name: strobe_reference_level_high
arguments.description: High reference level for the DDR read/write strobe.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract does not preserve a clean canonical header for this reference-level command.

description: This command sets or queries the DDR read/write reference level strobe mid value when the search type is DDR READWRITE.
shortDescription: Set or query DDR read/write strobe mid reference level

arguments.name: strobe_reference_level_mid
arguments.description: Mid reference level for the DDR read/write strobe.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract does not preserve a clean canonical header for this reference-level command.

description: Sets or queries the horizontal FastAcq maximum record length.
shortDescription: Set or query horizontal FastAcq maximum record length
notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor extract split the trailing VALue token with embedded whitespace.

description: Returns the total number of mask hits in the specified mask segment of the specified mask test.
shortDescription: Query mask hits for a specific segment

queryResponse.format: Returns the hit count for the selected mask segment.
queryResponse.description: The returned value is the number of waveform hits recorded in the specified segment.

examples.description: Might return :MASK:MASK1:SEG2:COUNT:HITS 211, indicating 211 hits in segment 2 of mask test 1.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor JSON reverses the SEG and COUNT mnemonic order.
The source-of-truth manual and tm_devices bindings both use MASK:MASK<x>:SEG<x>:COUNT:HITS?.

description: Returns the actual bandwidth for the current setup on the specified channel.
shortDescription: Query actual channel bandwidth

queryResponse.format: Returns the actual channel bandwidth for the current setup.
queryResponse.description: The returned value reflects the realized bandwidth after the current channel configuration and accessories are applied.

examples.description: Query the actual realized bandwidth on channel 1.

notes: Added as a manual override because the command-group table preserves this header while the vendor JSON does not expose a clean canonical record for it.

description: This command reboots the scope.
shortDescription: Reboot the scope application
notes: Added as a manual override because the real command uses a space, not a colon.
The vendor extract stores this in malformed example text instead of a clean canonical record.

description: Sets the plot response type.
shortDescription: Set or query plot response type

examples.description: Set plot 1 response type to phase.
Query the current response type for plot 1.

notes: Added as a manual override because the command-group table and tm_devices bindings confirm the canonical header while the vendor JSON does not expose a clean record.

description: Sets or queries the cursor function or mode.
shortDescription: Set or query cursor function
notes: Added as a manual override because the tm_devices bindings preserve the canonical cursor function header while the vendor JSON does not expose a clean modern-scope record.

description: Sets or queries the waveform cursor source.
shortDescription: Set or query waveform cursor source

examples.description: Use CH1 as the waveform cursor source.

notes: Added as a manual override because the tm_devices bindings preserve the canonical waveform cursor source header while the vendor JSON does not expose a clean modern-scope record.

description: Queries the horizontal delta between the waveform cursors.
shortDescription: Query waveform cursor horizontal delta

examples.description: Read horizontal delta between waveform cursors.

notes: Added as a manual override because the tm_devices bindings preserve this canonical query while the vendor JSON does not expose a clean modern-scope record.

description: Queries the vertical delta between the waveform cursors.
shortDescription: Query waveform cursor vertical delta

examples.description: Read vertical delta between waveform cursors.

notes: Added as a manual override because the tm_devices bindings preserve this canonical query while the vendor JSON does not expose a clean modern-scope record.

description: This command sets or queries the Spectrum View span for all channels in the Spectrum View display. The span is the range of frequencies observed around the center frequency.
shortDescription: Set or query Spectrum View span

examples.description: Set Spectrum View span to 200 MHz.
Query the current Spectrum View span.

notes: Added as a manual override because the vendor command JSON only exposes related query-side span commands while the tm_devices command corpus preserves the canonical Spectrum View span header.
Use with CH<x>:SV:CENTERFrequency for per-channel center frequency and the global SV:SPAN command for the display span.

description: Returns the measurement type for the specified measurement slot. Use to identify what type of measurement (FREQUENCY, PERIOD, IMDAPOWERQUALITY, IMDAEFFICIENCY, JITTERSUMMARY, etc.) is configured in each slot.
shortDescription: Query measurement type for a given measurement slot

description: Returns a comma-separated list of all active measurement slots (e.g. MEAS1,MEAS2,MEAS3). Use this to discover which measurements exist before reading results or adding new ones.
shortDescription: Query list of all active measurement slots

description: Sets or queries the IMDA measurement type. IMDA types include: IMDAPOWERQUALITY (3-phase power quality with phasor diagram), IMDAEFFICIENCY (motor efficiency), IMDAHARMONICS (harmonic analysis), IMDATORQUE (torque measurement), IMDASPEED (speed measurement), IMDASYSEFF (system efficiency), IMDAANGLE (angle), IMDADIRECTION (direction), IMDADQ0 (DQ0 transform), IMDAMECHPWR (mechanical power).
shortDescription: Set or query IMDA measurement type (power quality, efficiency, harmonics, etc.)

description: Returns the mean value from the current acquisition for the specified measurement. Works for both standard measurements (FREQUENCY, PERIOD, etc.) and IMDA measurements. For IMDA Power Quality, this returns the summary mean value.
shortDescription: Query mean result value for current acquisition

description: Returns the maximum value from the current acquisition for the specified measurement.
shortDescription: Query maximum result value for current acquisition

description: Returns the minimum value from the current acquisition for the specified measurement.
shortDescription: Query minimum result value for current acquisition

description: Returns the mean value across all acquisitions for the specified measurement.
shortDescription: Query mean result value across all acquisitions

description: Sets or queries the source for an edge trigger. In addition to standard analog channels, digital channels, LINE, and AUXiliary, Spectrum View derived traces can also be used as trigger sources when Spectrum View is active. The scope accepts CH<x>_MAG (Magnitude vs. Time), CH<x>_FREQ (Frequency vs. Time), and CH<x>_PHASE (Phase vs. Time) as extended trigger source identifiers. When queried, CH<x>_MAG is returned as CH<x>_MAG_VS_TIME.
shortDescription: Set or query edge trigger source (includes Spectrum View trace sources)

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: Trigger source. Standard sources: CH<x>, CH<x>_D<y>, LINE, AUXiliary. Spectrum View sources (when SV active): CH<x>_MAG (Magnitude vs Time, returned as CH<x>_MAG_VS_TIME), CH<x>_FREQ (Frequency vs Time), CH<x>_PHASE (Phase vs Time).

validValues.pattern: CH<x> | CH<x>_D<y> | LINE | AUXiliary | CH<x>_MAG | CH<x>_FREQ | CH<x>_PHASE

examples.description: Set edge trigger source to Channel 1.
Set edge trigger source to Channel 1 Spectrum View Magnitude vs Time trace. Scope returns CH1_MAG_VS_TIME when queried.
Query the current edge trigger source.

notes: UNDOCUMENTED: CH<x>_MAG, CH<x>_FREQ, CH<x>_PHASE trigger sources are accepted by MSO4/5/6/7 when Spectrum View is active but are not listed in the official SCPI programmer manual.
When queried, CH<x>_MAG is returned as CH<x>_MAG_VS_TIME by the scope firmware.
Requires SV:CH<x>:SELect:RF_MAGnitude ON (or equivalent) to be set first.
Discovered via real-world testing on MSO6B firmware.

description: Enables or disables the RF Magnitude vs Time trace for the specified channel's Spectrum View display. When ON, the Magnitude vs Time derived trace is visible and can be used as a trigger source (CH<x>_MAG).
shortDescription: Enable/disable Spectrum View Magnitude vs Time trace

examples.description: Enable Magnitude vs Time trace for Channel 1 Spectrum View.
Query if Magnitude vs Time trace is enabled for Channel 1.

notes: Must be ON before using CH<x>_MAG as a trigger source.
Part of the Spectrum View derived trace family alongside RF_FREQuency and RF_PHASe.

description: This command sets or queries the measurement source. The measurement number and source number are specified by x.
shortDescription: Set or query measurement source channel

arguments.name: measurement
arguments.description: MEAS<x> where x is measurement number (1-8)

description: This command sets or queries the upper threshold for a runt trigger. Use this command along with TRIGger:{A|B}:RUNT:THReshold:LOW to define the runt pulse boundaries.
shortDescription: Set or query upper runt trigger threshold voltage

description: This command sets or queries the lower threshold for a runt trigger. Use this command along with TRIGger:{A|B}:RUNT:THReshold:HIGH to define the runt pulse boundaries.
shortDescription: Set or query lower runt trigger threshold voltage

description: This command sets or queries the frequency offset for the specified Spectrum View channel. The offset shifts the displayed frequency axis.
shortDescription: Set or query Spectrum View channel frequency offset

arguments.name: channel
arguments.description: CH<x> where x is the channel number
